title: inset
description: Shorthand for top, right, bottom, and left on a positioned element — the same syntax as margin and padding. inset: 0 fills the parent completely in one declaration. Baseline widely available since 2021.
html:
Image title

This overlay fills the card completely using inset: 0
challenge: The overlay currently uses four separate position properties. Replace top, right, bottom, and left with a single inset: 0 declaration.

alternatives.name: Partial inset values
alternatives.description: Like margin and padding, inset accepts 1–4 values. inset: 1rem 0 sets 1rem top and bottom, 0 left and right — useful for an overlay that has breathing room at the edges.

realWorldUsage: Absolutely positioned overlays on images and cards, pseudo-element fills (position: absolute; inset: 0 on ::before or ::after), and any positioned element that should completely cover its parent. Also available as logical variants: inset-inline and inset-block.
